CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed

      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\enrichment.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * enrichment.ts — Shared dynamic enrichment logic (NotebookLM routing,\\n * wiki retrieval, memory recall) used by both the main agent turn and\\n * specialist proxy/delegation.\\n */\\n\\nimport { isEmbedRouterReady, routeByEmbedding } from \\\"./embed-router.js\\\";\\nimport { isWikiRetrievalReady, retrieveWikiPages, formatWikiBlock } from \\\"./wiki-retrieval.js\\\";\\nimport { isMemoryRecallReady, recallRelevantFacts, formatRecallBlock } from \\\"./memory-recall.js\\\";\\nimport { isIndexRetrievalReady, retrieveIndexChunks, formatIndexBlock } from \\\"./index-retrieval.js\\\";\\nimport { logHit } from \\\"./hit-log.js\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Scope filter — limits enrichment to a specialist's domain. */\\nexport interface EnrichmentScope {\\n  /** Only match these wiki page slugs. */\\n  wikiPages?: string[];\\n  /** Only match notebooks whose title is in this list. */\\n  notebooks?: string[];\\n  /** Only recall from these topic names. */\\n  topics?: string[];\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Run the three enrichment stages and return a prefix string to prepend\\n * to the user message. If `scope` is provided, results are filtered to\\n * only the specified domain (for specialist use).\\n */\\nexport async function runEnrichment(\\n  userMsg: string,\\n  userId: number | string,\\n  recentFacts?: Set<string>,\\n  scope?: EnrichmentScope,\\n): Promise<string> {\\n  let prefix = \\\"\\\";\\n\\n  // 1. NotebookLM routing\\n  if (isEmbedRouterReady()) {\\n    try {\\n      const titleFilter = scope?.notebooks?.length\\n        ? new Set(scope.notebooks)\\n        : undefined;\\n      const hits = await routeByEmbedding(userMsg, titleFilter);\\n      if (hits.length > 0) {\\n        const hitLines = hits.map(\\n          (h) => `- 《${h.title}》(id=${h.notebookId}, score=${h.score.toFixed(3)}, strategy=${h.strategy})`,\\n        );\\n        prefix +=\\n          `[NotebookLM routing — embedding match]\\\\n` +\\n          `以下 notebook 與使用者問題語意相近，請用 notebook_query 查詢後以該 strategy 格式回答：\\\\n` +\\n          hitLines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n          `\\\\n[End routing]\\\\n\\\\n`;\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] notebooklm routing failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // 2. Wiki retrieval\\n  const wikiHitSlugs = new Set<string>();\\n  if (isWikiRetrievalReady()) {\\n    try {\\n      const slugFilter = scope?.wikiPages?.length\\n        ? new Set(scope.wikiPages)\\n        : undefined;\\n      const hits = await retrieveWikiPages(userMsg, slugFilter);\\n      // Dedup: skip wiki pages already injected in a prior turn\\n      const fresh = recentFacts\\n        ? hits.filter((h) => !recentFacts.has(`wiki:${h.slug}`))\\n        : hits;\\n      if (fresh.length > 0) {\\n        for (const h of fresh) {\\n          wikiHitSlugs.add(h.slug);\\n          recentFacts?.add(`wiki:${h.slug}`);\\n          logHit(\\\"wiki\\\", h.slug, \\\"wiki-retrieval\\\");\\n        }\\n        prefix = formatWikiBlock(fresh) + prefix;\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] wiki retrieval failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // 3. Memory recall\\n  if (isMemoryRecallReady()) {\\n    try {\\n      const includeTopics = scope?.topics?.length\\n        ? new Set(scope.topics)\\n        : undefined;\\n      const hits = await recallRelevantFacts(\\n        userMsg, userId, recentFacts, wikiHitSlugs, includeTopics,\\n      );\\n      if (hits.length > 0) {\\n        // Track injected facts for cross-turn dedup\\n        if (recentFacts) {\\n          for (const h of hits) for (const f of h.facts) recentFacts.add(f);\\n        }\\n        for (const h of hits) logHit(\\\"fact\\\", h.topic, \\\"memory-recall\\\");\\n        prefix = formatRecallBlock(hits) + prefix;\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] memory recall failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // 4. Index retrieval (PageIndex tree-based document chunks)\\n  if (isIndexRetrievalReady() && !scope) {\\n    try {\\n      const hits = await retrieveIndexChunks(userMsg);\\n      const fresh = recentFacts\\n        ? hits.filter((h) => !recentFacts.has(`idx:${h.slug}`))\\n        : hits;\\n      if (fresh.length > 0) {\\n        for (const h of fresh) {\\n          recentFacts?.add(`idx:${h.slug}`);\\n          logHit(\\\"index\\\", h.slug, \\\"index-retrieval\\\");\\n        }\\n        prefix += formatIndexBlock(fresh);\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] index retrieval failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return applyPrefixCap(prefix);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Enrichment Prefix Cap ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Section markers used to identify removable blocks in priority order (lowest first).\\n * When the enrichment prefix exceeds the character cap, sections are stripped\\n * starting from the lowest-priority one until it fits.\\n */\\nconst SECTION_PRIORITY: Array<{ start: string; end: string; label: string }> = [\\n  { start: \\\"[Document indexes\\\", end: \\\"[End indexes]\\\", label: \\\"index-retrieval\\\" },\\n  { start: \\\"[Memory recall\\\", end: \\\"[End memory recall]\\\", label: \\\"memory-recall\\\" },\\n  { start: \\\"[Wiki retrieval\\\", end: \\\"[End wiki retrieval]\\\", label: \\\"wiki-retrieval\\\" },\\n  // NotebookLM routing and Skill routing are kept (short + high-value routing directives)\\n];\\n\\n/**\\n * If the prefix exceeds config.enrichmentPrefixMaxChars, strip lowest-priority\\n * sections until it fits. Returns the (possibly trimmed) prefix.\\n */\\nfunction applyPrefixCap(prefix: string): string {\\n  const cap = config.enrichmentPrefixMaxChars;\\n  if (prefix.length <= cap) return prefix;\\n\\n  let result = prefix;\\n  for (const section of SECTION_PRIORITY) {\\n    if (result.length <= cap) break;\\n\\n    const startIdx = result.indexOf(section.start);\\n    if (startIdx < 0) continue;\\n    const endIdx = result.indexOf(section.end, startIdx);\\n    if (endIdx < 0) continue;\\n\\n    const removed = result.slice(startIdx, endIdx + section.end.length);\\n    result = result.slice(0, startIdx) + result.slice(endIdx + section.end.length);\\n    // Clean up leading/trailing newlines from removal\\n    result = result.replace(/\\\\n{3,}/g, \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\");\\n\\n    console.log(\\n      `[enrichment] prefix cap: stripped [${section.label}] (${removed.length} chars), ` +\\n      `remaining ${result.length}/${cap}`,\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  // If still over cap after stripping all removable sections, hard-truncate\\n  if (result.length > cap) {\\n    console.warn(\\n      `[enrichment] prefix still over cap after stripping (${result.length}/${cap}), hard-truncating`,\\n    );\\n    result = result.slice(0, cap) + \\\"\\\\n[...enrichment truncated]\\\\n\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  return result;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":184,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":184}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\hit-log.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * hit-log.ts — Track usage of facts/wiki/skills via append-only JSONL.\\n * Provides stats for factlint \\\"zero-hit\\\" reporting and grouped evaluation.\\n */\\nimport { appendFile, readFile } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport { readFileSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\nconst LOG_FILE = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"hit-log.jsonl\\\");\\n\\nexport interface HitEntry {\\n  ts: string;\\n  type: \\\"fact\\\" | \\\"wiki\\\" | \\\"skill\\\" | \\\"index\\\";\\n  key: string;\\n  source: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Append a hit entry. Fire-and-forget (never throws). */\\nexport function logHit(type: HitEntry[\\\"type\\\"], key: string, source: string): void {\\n  const entry: HitEntry = { ts: new Date().toISOString(), type, key, source };\\n  appendFile(LOG_FILE, JSON.stringify(entry) + \\\"\\\\n\\\").catch(() => {});\\n}\\n\\n/** Read hit log and return stats for the last N days. */\\nexport async function getHitStats(days = 30): Promise<Map<string, number>> {\\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - days * 86_400_000;\\n  const stats = new Map<string, number>();\\n  let raw: string;\\n  try {\\n    raw = await readFile(LOG_FILE, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  } catch {\\n    return stats;\\n  }\\n  for (const line of raw.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\")) {\\n    if (!line.trim()) continue;\\n    try {\\n      const e: HitEntry = JSON.parse(line);\\n      if (new Date(e.ts).getTime() < cutoff) continue;\\n      const k = `${e.type}:${e.key}`;\\n      stats.set(k, (stats.get(k) ?? 0) + 1);\\n    } catch { /* skip malformed */ }\\n  }\\n  return stats;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Repo Health (Compression Metric) ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ACTIONABLE_RE = /因為|避免|不要|不可|遇到.*要|必須|禁止|改用|換成|確保|注意/;\\n\\nexport interface RepoHealth {\\n  total: number;\\n  actionable: number;\\n  ratio: number;\\n}\\n\\n/** Compute repo health: ratio of total facts to actionable (guidance-bearing) facts. */\\nexport function computeRepoHealth(shards: Array<{ file: string }>): RepoHealth {\\n  let total = 0, actionable = 0;\\n  for (const s of shards) {\\n    let content: string;\\n    try { content = readFileSync(s.file, \\\"utf8\\\"); } catch { continue; }\\n    for (const line of content.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)) {\\n      if (!line.trim() || !line.startsWith(\\\"-\\\")) continue;\\n      total++;\\n      if (ACTIONABLE_RE.test(line)) actionable++;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return { total, actionable, ratio: actionable > 0 ? total / actionable : total };\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":71,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":71}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Memory recall\\n  if (isMemoryRecallReady()) {\\n    try {\\n      const includeTopics = scope?.topics?.length\\n        ? new Set(scope.topics)\\n        : undefined;\\n      const hits = await recallRelevantFacts(\\n        userMsg, userId, recentFacts, wikiHitSlugs, includeTopics,\\n      );\\n      if (hits.length > 0) {\\n        // Track injected facts for cross-turn dedup\\n        if (recentFacts) {\\n          for (const h of hits) for (const f of h.facts) recentFacts.add(f);\\n        }\\n        for (const h of hits) logHit(\\\"fact\\\", h.topic, \\\"memory-recall\\\");\\n        prefix = formatRecallBlock(hits) + prefix;\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] memory recall failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // 4. Index retrieval (PageIndex tree-based document chunks)\\n  if (isIndexRetrievalReady() && !scope) {\\n    try {\\n      const hits = await retrieveIndexChunks(userMsg);\\n      const fresh = recentFacts\\n        ? hits.filter((h) => !recentFacts.has(`idx:${h.slug}`))\\n        : hits;\\n      if (fresh.length > 0) {\\n        for (const h of fresh) {\\n          recentFacts?.add(`idx:${h.slug}`);\\n          logHit(\\\"index\\\", h.slug, \\\"index-retrieval\\\");\\n        }\\n        prefix += formatIndexBlock(fresh);\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(\\\"[enrichment] index retrieval failed:\\\", err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return applyPrefixCap(prefix);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Enrichment Prefix Cap ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Section markers used to identify removable blocks in priority order (lowest first).\\n * When the enrichment prefix exceeds the character cap, sections are stripped\\n * starting from the lowest-priority one until it fits.\\n */\\nconst SECTION_PRIORITY: Array<{ start: string; end: string; label: string }> = [\\n  { start: \\\"[Document indexes\\\", end: \\\"[End indexes]\\\", label: \\\"index-retrieval\\\" },\\n  { start: \\\"[Memory recall\\\", end: \\\"[End memory recall]\\\", label: \\\"memory-recall\\\" },\\n  { start: \\\"[Wiki retrieval\\\", end: \\\"[End wiki retrieval]\\\", label: \\\"wiki-retrieval\\\" },\\n  // NotebookLM routing and Skill routing are kept (short + high-value routing directives)\\n];\\n\\n/**\\n * If the prefix exceeds config.enrichmentPrefixMaxChars, strip lowest-priority\\n * sections until it fits. Returns the (possibly trimmed) prefix.\\n */\\nfunction applyPrefixCap(prefix: string): string {\\n  const cap = config.enrichmentPrefixMaxChars;\\n  if (prefix.length <= cap) return prefix;\\n\\n  let result = prefix;\\n  for (const section of SECTION_PRIORITY) {\\n    if (result.length <= cap) break;\\n\\n    const startIdx = result.indexOf(section.start);\\n    if (startIdx < 0) continue;\\n    const endIdx = result.indexOf(section.end, startIdx);\\n    if (endIdx < 0) continue;\\n\\n    const removed = result.slice(startIdx, endIdx + section.end.length);\\n    result = result.slice(0, startIdx) + result.slice(endIdx + section.end.length);\\n    // Clean up leading/trailing newlines from removal\\n    result = result.replace(/\\\\n{3,}/g, \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\");\\n\\n    console.log(\\n      `[enrichment] prefix cap: stripped [${section.label}] (${removed.length} chars), ` +\\n      `remaining ${result.length}/${cap}`,\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  // If still over cap after stripping all removable sections, hard-truncate\\n  if (result.length > cap) {\\n    console.warn(\\n      `[enrichment] prefix still over cap after stripping (${result.length}/${cap}), hard-truncating`,\\n    );\\n    result = result.slice(0, cap) + \\\"\\\\n[...enrichment truncated]\\\\n\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  return result;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":184,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":184}}"</outcome>

Fire-and-forget (never throws). */\\nexport function logHit(type: HitEntry[\\\"type\\\"], key: string, source: string): void {\\n  const entry: HitEntry = { ts: new Date().toISOString(), type, key, source };\\n  appendFile(LOG_FILE, JSON.stringify(entry) + \\\"\\\\n\\\").catch(() => {});\\n}\\n\\n/** Read hit log and return stats for the last N days. */\\nexport async function getHitStats(days = 30): Promise<Map<string, number>> {\\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - days * 86_400_000;\\n  const stats = new Map<string, number>();\\n  let raw: string;\\n  try {\\n    raw = await readFile(LOG_FILE, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  } catch {\\n    return stats;\\n  }\\n  for (const line of raw.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\")) {\\n    if (!line.trim()) continue;\\n    try {\\n      const e: HitEntry = JSON.parse(line);\\n      if (new Date(e.ts).getTime() < cutoff) continue;\\n      const k = `${e.type}:${e.key}`;\\n      stats.set(k, (stats.get(k) ?? 0) + 1);\\n    } catch { /* skip malformed */ }\\n  }\\n  return stats;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Repo Health (Compression Metric) ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ACTIONABLE_RE = /因為|避免|不要|不可|遇到.*要|必須|禁止|改用|換成|確保|注意/;\\n\\nexport interface RepoHealth {\\n  total: number;\\n  actionable: number;\\n  ratio: number;\\n}\\n\\n/** Compute repo health: ratio of total facts to actionable (guidance-bearing) facts. */\\nexport function computeRepoHealth(shards: Array<{ file: string }>): RepoHealth {\\n  let total = 0, actionable = 0;\\n  for (const s of shards) {\\n    let content: string;\\n    try { content = readFileSync(s.file, \\\"utf8\\\"); } catch { continue; }\\n    for (const line of content.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)) {\\n      if (!line.trim() || !line.startsWith(\\\"-\\\")) continue;\\n      total++;\\n      if (ACTIONABLE_RE.test(line)) actionable++;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return { total, actionable, ratio: actionable > 0 ? total / actionable : total };\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":71,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":71}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is looking at P3 which is about "hit-log fact 接線" (hit-log fact wiring).

From the observations:
1. They grepped for `logHit` and found it's already being called in enrichment.ts line 102: `logHit("fact", h.topic, "memory-recall")` when facts are recalled
2. They read enrichment.ts to see the full context
3. They read hit-log.ts to understand the hit-log system
4. They grepped memory-recall.ts to understand the recall flow

It looks like they're investigating whether fact hits are already being logged. From enrichment.ts line 102, facts ARE being logged when recalled. But the question is whether there are other places where facts should be logged but aren't yet.

The hit-log system tracks:
- type: "fact" | "wiki" | "skill" | "index"
- key: string
- source: string

Current fact logging only happens on memory-recall. But facts could potentially be:
1. Created (new facts added)
2. Updated (facts modified)
3. Recalled (already logged ✓)
4. Manually referenced

So the investigation is likely checking if fact creation/updates also need to be logged, not just recalls.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Hit-log fact tracking already implemented for recalls</title>
  <subtitle>Memory recall already logs fact hits via logHit("fact", topic, "memory-recall") in enrichment flow</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>enrichment.ts line 102 logs fact hits when memory recall injects facts into context</fact>
    <fact>logHit tracks four types: fact, wiki, skill, index via append-only JSONL in memory directory</fact>
    <fact>Hit-log system stores timestamp, type, key (topic name for facts), and source string per entry</fact>
    <fact>getHitStats function reads hit-log.jsonl and computes frequency statistics for last N days</fact>
    <fact>Hit stats power factlint zero-hit reporting and /memory decay detection (60-day threshold)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation of P3 (hit-log fact wiring) revealed that fact tracking via hit-log is already operational for the memory recall flow. When recallRelevantFacts returns topic-based fact matches, enrichment.ts logs each hit with logHit("fact", h.topic, "memory-recall") before formatting the recall block. The hit-log system uses an append-only JSONL file that records timestamp, type, key (topic for facts), and source. These logs feed into factlint's zero-hit detection and /memory's 60-day decay checks. The open question is whether other fact lifecycle events (creation, updates, manual edits) should also be logged, or if recall-only tracking is sufficient for the current use case.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/enrichment.ts</file>
    <file>src/hit-log.ts</file>
    <file>src/memory-recall.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Memory recall initialization sequence verified</title>
  <subtitle>initMemoryRecall runs after initEmbedModel in index.ts startup, building topic vectors for semantic matching</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts calls initEmbedModel at line 1391 then initMemoryRecall at line 1425 during startup</fact>
    <fact>initMemoryRecall checks isEmbedModelReady before building topic vectors from topics.json keywords</fact>
    <fact>memory-recall.ts creates one embedding vector per topic by encoding "topic-name: keyword1, keyword2, keyword3"</fact>
    <fact>recallRelevantFacts uses cosine similarity between query embedding and topic vectors to select relevant facts</fact>
    <fact>Multiple subsystems depend on isEmbedModelReady: embed-router, wiki-retrieval, index-retrieval, skill-router, intent-classifier</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Confirmed the initialization flow for memory recall and its dependency on the embedding model. During startup, index.ts loads the embedding model first (initEmbedModel), then builds topic vectors from topics.json rules (initMemoryRecall). Each topic's keywords are combined into a representative string and embedded for semantic search. When recallRelevantFacts runs during enrichment, it embeds the user message and uses cosine similarity against these pre-computed topic vectors to select relevant fact shards. The embedding model is shared across multiple semantic subsystems (wiki, index, skill routing, intent classification), making the initialization order critical. This confirms that fact recall logging happens at the right layer (after successful semantic matching) and suggests no additional initialization-time logging is needed for P3.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Fact sync to database happens at startup without hit-log tracking</title>
  <subtitle>syncFactsToDb inserts/deletes facts from markdown to SQLite during initialization but does not log these events</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts startup sequence initializes components in order: embed model → memory DB → memory recall → skill router → wiki retrieval → index retrieval</fact>
    <fact>syncFactsToDb runs after initMemoryDb at startup, reporting inserted and deleted fact counts</fact>
    <fact>syncFactsToDb is a batch operation that syncs markdown facts to SQLite but does not call logHit</fact>
    <fact>Only memory recall (enrichment.ts line 102) logs fact hits via logHit("fact", topic, "memory-recall")</fact>
    <fact>Hit-log currently tracks fact usage (recalls) but not fact lifecycle events (creation, sync, updates, deletions)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The startup initialization sequence reveals a potential gap in fact tracking. When the bot starts, syncFactsToDb performs a batch sync from markdown files to the SQLite memory database, reporting how many facts were inserted or deleted. However, this sync operation does not log to hit-log.jsonl. Currently, only the memory recall flow (when facts are retrieved during enrichment) logs hits. This means hit-log tracks fact usage but not fact lifecycle events. P3 investigation suggests the question is whether fact creation, updates, and deletions should also be logged to hit-log for completeness, or whether recall-only tracking is sufficient for the intended analytics use cases (zero-hit detection, decay analysis).</narrative>

content.thinking:
This is very revealing. The primary session is looking at index.ts lines 1150-1260, and I can see that the main conversation handler does NOT call runEnrichment() at all. Instead, it has inline enrichment logic:

1. NotebookLM routing
2. Wiki retrieval (but NO logHit call here - line ~1168 shows formatWikiBlock but no logHit)
3. Memory recall (line ~1176-1185, also NO logHit call)
4. Skill routing (line ~1197 DOES call logHit("skill", h.name, "skill-routing"))
5. Intent classification

So the main index.ts conversation handler does enrichment inline without logging hits for wiki or facts, while only the specialist.ts code path (lines shown earlier) uses runEnrichment() which DOES have the logHit calls.

This explains why there are ZERO fact hits in hit-log.jsonl - the main conversation path doesn't call logHit for facts, only the specialist proxy path does. And apparently the specialist path hasn't been exercised yet in the logged period.

This is a critical discovery - there's an inconsistency between the main enrichment path (index.ts inline) and the specialist enrichment path (enrichment.ts runEnrichment function). The runEnrichment function has logHit calls, but the main path doesn't.

  <narrative>Root cause identified for missing fact hits in hit-log.jsonl. The codebase has two enrichment paths: (1) index.ts main conversation handler performs inline enrichment with wiki retrieval and memory recall but only logs skill hits, and (2) specialist.ts delegation flows call the centralized runEnrichment function which properly logs all hit types (wiki, facts, index, skills). The inline path handles most conversations, explaining why hit-log.jsonl contains 40 skill entries but zero fact/wiki entries. The specialist path with proper logging has not been invoked during the logged period. P3's actual scope is now clear: unify enrichment logging by either migrating the main path to use runEnrichment, or adding missing logHit calls to the inline enrichment code.</narrative>

So the situation is:

- Specialist path (specialist.ts): uses runEnrichment which has wiki, memory recall, index retrieval, and logs all hit types
- Main path is missing: logHit for wiki, logHit for facts, and doesn't have index retrieval at all

This confirms P3 is about adding missing hit logging to the main conversation path.

  <narrative>Further investigation reveals the main conversation path (index.ts) is missing not only hit logging for wiki and facts but also the entire index retrieval enrichment stage. The inline enrichment code has three stages (wiki, memory recall, skill routing) while the centralized runEnrichment function has four (NotebookLM routing, wiki, memory recall, index retrieval). Only skill routing logs hits in the main path. The specialist path that uses runEnrichment has full coverage but represents a minority of conversations. P3's full scope is now clear: the main conversation path needs (1) logHit calls added for wiki retrieval and memory recall, and (2) index retrieval stage integrated with its logHit call, bringing it to parity with the specialist path's runEnrichment function.</narrative>

Policy:\\n      //   Q1 scope:  pending-only. We only auto-resume the chat that\\n      //              triggered /restart (marker.chatId).
